title: lichen_l2_enable re-init path assumes lora_l2 was initialized
description:
lichen_l2.c:265-275: When re-enabling after disable, lichen_l2_enable() calls lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() which returns NULL if lora_l2 was never initialized (or init failed).

The code handles NULL at line 266-269, but the error message 'Failed to get EUI-64 for link_ctx re-init' doesn't indicate the actual problem (lora_l2 not initialized).

What goes wrong: User sees 'Failed to get EUI-64 for link_ctx re-init' when the real issue is that the underlying LoRa layer failed to initialize. Confusing diagnostic that will generate support requests.

The enable path then calls lichen_lora_l2_start() at line 283, which WILL check initialized and return -EINVAL with 'LoRa L2 not initialized' log. So the actual error is eventually surfaced, but after a misleading earlier message.

Fix: Either (1) check lichen_lora_l2_is_running() || atomic_get(&lora_state.initialized) explicitly, or (2) improve the error message to suggest checking LoRa initialization.
close_reason: Fixed: improved error message at line 282 to say 'Cannot re-init link_ctx: LoRa L2 not initialized' which accurately reflects the root cause (lora_l2 not initialized) rather than the symptom (failed to get EUI-64). Added comment noting that the callee already logs the specific error.
